diff --git a/include/simdjson/generic/ondemand/parser.h b/include/simdjson/generic/ondemand/parser.h

excluded_defs.name: load
excluded_defs.reason: Read as the reference API pattern to mirror; not edited

exploration_log.aha_moment: dom::parser::load() uses a stored padded_string buffer (loaded_bytes) to keep the data alive during iteration. For ondemand, I can add a `_loaded_json` unique_ptr that holds the padded_string, and call `iterate(*_loaded_json)` on it.
exploration_log.hindsight: Using `unique_ptr<padded_string>` avoids needing to include padded_string.h in the header (forward declaration suffices for pointer member). A simpler approach would have been to store a bare `padded_string` but that requires including the full definition.

alternatives: Store padded_string by value (requires full definition in header)
key_decisions.decision: Add load(std::string_view path) method storing the padded_string in a unique_ptr<padded_string> member
key_decisions.reasoning: load() matches the dom::parser naming; unique_ptr<padded_string> keeps the header forward-declaration-compatible

queries.query_text: Add load() to ondemand parser.h and parser-inl.h using padded_string::load() + iterate()

solve_notes: Added `load(std::string_view path)` to `ondemand::parser`. The implementation loads the file into a `padded_string` via `padded_string::load()`, stores it in a `unique_ptr<padded_string>` member `_loaded_json` (to keep the buffer alive during iteration), then delegates to `iterate(*_loaded_json)`. The declaration and private field were added to `parser.h`; the implementation was added to `parser-inl.h`.
task_text: The `dom::parser` exposes a `load(std::string_view path)` method that reads a file and returns a parsed element in a single call. The `ondemand::parser` provides no equivalent; users must manually call `padded_string::load(path)` and then pass the result to `iterate()`. Add an `iterate(std::string_view path)` overload (or a `load()` method) to `ondemand::parser` in `include/simdjson/generic/ondemand/parser.h` and `parser-inl.h` that loads the file into a `padded_string` and returns a `simdjson_result<document>`, matching the convenience of the DOM API.
